source_reviews.notes: The exact URL resolves to the official Census NAICS search interface, but its current static rendering does not expose the requested 541921 detail text. It establishes the authoritative NAICS system but does not presently display the registered scope wording in the reopened text view.

The page identifies a worldwide sample of more than 1,000 AI-adopting photographers and supports automated culling/editing/retouching, faster workflow, 60% emphasizing premium experiences, and 70% reporting greater price sensitivity. The packet correctly discloses vendor sponsorship, selection, geography, and the absence of representative penetration evidence.

The page supports the stated 2025 PPA survey frame of 443 working professionals with household income over $75,000 who hired a photographer in the prior three years, together with positive headshot budget and experience signals. It is used only for narrative terminal-demand context.

The adviser page states a 1.5-3.0x SDE range, distinguishes SDE from EBITDA, and identifies owner dependence, commercial/client relationships, associate photographers, brand, and transferable systems as value drivers. The packet preserves the source's marketing/adviser and SDE-versus-EBITDA limitations.

The announcement supports the $825 million 2018 acquisition agreement, Lifetouch's national school-photography leadership, its scale, and the distinct contracted volume-photography segment. The record correctly treats it as historical and non-comparable to a typical target.

findings.explanation: The exact registered Census URL is live, but its current static page rendering does not expose the 541921 definition that the source registry says it supports.
findings.materiality: This is non-fatal because the page-state limitation affects scope presentation rather than the low capture, zero multiple-arbitrage score, or kill thesis; it does not plausibly reverse a factor direction or publication conclusion.
findings.remediation: In a future research attempt, register a stable Census code-definition page or downloadable table whose static content exposes the 541921 description.
findings.publication_caveat: The registered Census 541921 URL currently resolves to the official NAICS search interface without exposing the cited definition in its static page text.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The source is a vendor-sponsored, AI-adopter survey rather than a representative US portrait-studio time-and-motion study.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Generative substitution of the purchased portrait is treated in terminal demand and capture, not as labor replacement.
inputs.pi_dist: Based on a vendor survey of AI adopters, not observed studio margin retention.
inputs.pi_moat: The record has no US revenue split between contracted volume work and retail/founder-led work.
inputs.t50_years: This is workflow adoption, not replacement of physical portrait capture.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No representative US portrait-studio adoption census was found in the reopened sources.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Do not treat this estimate as evidence of a succession wave.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of documentation is not proof that no owner will retire.
inputs.active_consolidators: The acquisition is historical and its target is not a typical $1-10M EBITDA portrait studio.
inputs.buy_mult: The score requests EBITDA multiples but the available photography evidence is SDE-based marketing guidance, not closed $1-10M EBITDA transactions; this is an imperfect proxy.
inputs.exit_mult: The same SDE-versus-EBITDA mismatch applies; the Lifetouch transaction is a non-comparable national-volume benchmark.
inputs.pricing_structure: No code-wide revenue split is available in the reopened sources.
The registered Census 541921 URL currently resolves to the official NAICS search interface without exposing the cited definition in its static page text.
summary: Mechanics and exact-artifact binding pass. Reopened evidence supports workflow automation, weak retention/transferability, thin consolidation economics, and a pressured but not wholly melting demand profile. The kill thesis remains useful, with all authored limitations preserved and one additional non-fatal caveat about the exact Census URL's current static rendering.
